articles.title: Trump Calls Xi a ‘Friend.’ But He Left China Without Any Breakthroughs.
articles.summary: The lack of concrete agreements with Beijing shows the risks of President Trump’s personality-driven foreign policy, which rests on the belief that he can defend U.S. interests through charm and force

articles.title: Trump Uses Taiwan Arms Sales as Bargaining Chip With China, in a Risky Move
articles.summary: The president said a potential arms deal for Taiwan was a “very good negotiating chip” in talks with Beijing. His words raise questions about the reliability of U.S. support.

articles.title: China Will Host Putin, Days After Trump’s Visit
articles.summary: The Russian leader will go to Beijing on Tuesday for talks with President Xi Jinping. The Kremlin said he had watched President Trump’s visit closely.

articles.title: A new Ebola outbreak has already killed 87 people in Democratic Republic of Congo
articles.summary: Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.

articles.title: Voters in Louisiana Head to the Polls, Uncertain but Determined
articles.summary: Votes cast for the House candidates on Saturday’s primary ballot won’t count after state officials moved the election to November to provide time to redraw congressional maps.

articles.title: At Supreme Court, Virginia Democrats Pressed Legal Theory Embraced by Trump
articles.summary: A long-shot emergency request to restore a voting map was the latest salvo in an all-out legal war over control of the House.

articles.title: North America poised for light display of aurora borealis
articles.summary: The splashy nighttime phenomenon will be best observed on Saturday and Sunday nights

articles.title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
articles.summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

articles.title: In Congress, a Sexual Harassment ‘Minefield’ Persists, Aides and Former Staff Say
articles.summary: Nearly 10 years after Congress instituted measures to make it easier for women to lodge harassment complaints, lawmakers and aides say the behavior is still rampant.

articles.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead off Danish coast
articles.summary: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded o

articles.title: This Republican voted to convict Trump. Now he's up for reelection. Can he survive?
articles.summary: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.

articles.title: The war in Ukraine is at a standstill as societal fatigue grows in Russia
articles.summary: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.

articles.title: Elephants eat their crops. Farmers strike back. It's a war that's only getting worse
articles.summary: In Sri Lanka, the islanders revere elephants. But for farmers, there's rising tension that's leading to more fatal encounters — for both humans and hungry pachyderms.

articles.title: The townspeople of Vilseck, Germany, worry that Trump may pull out 5,000 U.S. troops
articles.summary: President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.

articles.title: A Bitter Land Commissioner Race in New Mexico Over 9 Million Acres
articles.summary: A Republican candidate for the state land commissioner post has Democrats worried that protected state and federal land could be opened to industry.

articles.title: Colleges got more rural students to apply. The challenge is getting them to attend
articles.summary: Some of the nation's most selective institutions are slowly increasing their rural enrollment with the help of millions of dollars from a rural alumnus of the University of Chicago.

articles.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint U.S.-Nigerian mission
articles.summary: Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.

articles.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
articles.summary: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

articles.title: Israel says it killed the leader of Hamas' military wing
articles.summary: Israel says it has killed the leader of Hamas' military wing, one of the architects of the Oct, 7, 2023, attacks that triggered the war in Gaza.

articles.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
articles.summary: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.

articles.title: CIA director's visit to Cuba echoes past memories of U.S. in Latin America
articles.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes a surprise visit to Cuba as the spy agency calls on the nation to make "fundamental changes." Cuba says it has run out of fuel and is suffering massive blackouts.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
